Petition For Surcharge Of Former Trustee Tami Honda For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Continued To Mar-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Continued To Mar-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Raymond Honda (Petitioner).
Phillip Campbell for Tami Honda and Donald Honda.

Mr. Campbell stated that they met and conferred, that his Clients' responses to the Petition for Surcharge are out to the Clients for signature, that they talked about mediation, that the family house was sold and requested a 90-day continuance. Counsel stated that they will be using a mediator that is not on the Court's panel and that they will not be participating in Pro Bono Mediation. The Petition for Surcharge, etc., Petition for A Report of The Trust and Its Administration, etc. and status on mediation are continued to March 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.
Off Calendar Jul-31-2017 Continued To Aug-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Brillant appeared by telephone for Raymond Honda.
Phillip Campbell for Donny Honda (Current Trustee) and specially appearing for Tami Honda (Former Trustee).

Mr. Campbell stated that they are working with Accountant Kathleen Chattler for preparation of a five year accounting, that they have a near final draft, that notes were sent back to her last week and that he was here to ask for more time to get the accounting filed. Mr. Brillant stated that he is okay with a two week continuance, and that he wants 45-days after filing of the accounting to review it, respond to it and object to it. Mr. Campbell stated that distribution has been made, that Ms. Honda has sent copies of the bank statements and that Donny has been working on cleaning out the house. The Petition for Report of Trust; an Accounting, etc. is continued to August 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition is to be filed and served by August 10, 2017.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Continued To Jul-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for petitioner Raymond Honda; Philip Campbell for Donald Honda; Tami Honda, former trustee in pro per

Ms. Honda stated that she was working on an accounting, and that it should be ready in four weeks. The Court set a deadline of July 24, 2017 for the filing of the accounting, and set a status hearing re filing of accounting for July 31, 2017 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

The Court inquired regarding mediation status. Mr. Campbell reported that the house in El Sobrante was only half cleared out, and opined that it would be better to have the house cleared out and ready for sale and wrap up the trust before meaningful settlement discussions could take place. The Court set a deadline of November 17, 2017 for completing mediation, and set a status hearing re mediation for November 27, 2017 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Continued To Jun-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught for Raymond Honda.
Philip Campbell for Donny Honda (Trustee).
Tammy Honda (Former Trustee) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Honda stated that they are still working on the accounting and that she expects a draft to be completed by June 15th. Mr. Campbell stated that the value of the Trust is real estate in San Pablo and $100,000.00 in cash, that he thinks the value is under $3,000,000.00 and that he does not think mediation is necessary. Mr. Vaught stated that it is premature to send them to mediation and that mediation is a possibility after receiving the accounting. The Petition for a Report of the Trust and Its Administration, etc. is continued to June 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Honda to get the accounting done.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Continued To May-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for and with petitioner Robert Honda; Phil Campbell for and with trustee Donny Honda; former trustee Tami Honda.

Mr. Campbell reported that petitioner Robert Honda took over as trustee in July of 2016, and petitioner has filed an accounting for the period July 29, 2016-December 31, 2016. He stated his opinion that what is now needed is a filing of an accounting by former trustee Tami Honda for the period she was trustee. Mr. Vaught stated that the dates for which Tami was trustee were April 2011 through July 28, 2016. Tami Honda stated that she had found an accountant who was preparing an accounting for the period of November 1014 (when her mother died) through July of 2016. She said that she could provide the accountand with bank statements and other records from April 2011 onwards, and that she would try to locate those documents. The accountant had given her a 3-4 week estimate for the time to prepare the accounting for just the Nov. 2014-July 2016 period; she thought she would need a longer period to do a complete accounting. Mr. Campbell stated that there were still items of personal property in the house, and that the property needs to be cleared out and put up for sale. The Court inquired if it would be better to submit an accounting for the period from Nov. 2014-July 2016 and file a separate accounting for the period April 2011-Nov. 2014, of do one comprehensive accounting by the former trustee. Mr. Vaughtstated that things are moving along now that Mr. Campbell is involved, and that it made sense to him to wait and have a single accounting. The matter was continued for status hearing on Tami Honda's petition for final accounting on May 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition (1)For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration, (2)For An Accounting, Et Al.
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Continued To Mar-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
